titlestmt.titleproper: Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning records6079
titlestmt.author: Compiled by S.L.

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-07-13 18:22:14 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning (Corning, N.Y.)

did.abstract: Records of the Painted Post Circuit quarterly conference meeting (1832-1839) and the Painted Post Church (1832-1833); records of the leader's minutes for the Corning circuit, and quarterly conference meeting minutes for the Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning. Also Class Number One's class leader's records (1839-1844), Trustee's annual meeting minutes (1857-1897) and a register listing the history of, statistics about and a subscription list for the church built in 1861; marriage, probationer's, membership and class lists. Subjects discussed include slavery, temperance, a missionary society, revivals and church discipline.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In 1832 Painted Post was set off as a separate circuit containing (as of 1837) Little Flatts, Painted Post, "Addison Village", West Addison, Erwin Centre, Campbell and Tioga. In 1833 a class formed at Caton, which was set off with Corning in November 1839. Another division occurred in 1843 when Erwin, Painted Post and Campbell formed a circuit named after the latter two towns. In 1861-62, the Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning built a church which seated 700.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Item 1 contains records of the Painted Post Circuit Quarterly Conference Meeting (1832-1839, Parts A,D) and the Painted Post Church (Part C, 1832-33); records of the leader's minutes for the Coming Circuit (Part B, 1841-44); Quarterly Conference minutes for the Corning Circuit (Part E, 1839-1846) and Quarterly Conference meeting minutes for the Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning (Part F, December 1847-1867). The collection also includes Class No. 1's class leader's records (1839-1844); Trustee's annual meeting minutes (1857-1897); and a register listing the history of, statistics about and a subscription list for the church built in 1861; marriage, probationer's records, and class and membership lists. Subjects discussed include slavery, temperance, a missionary society, revivals and church discipline.
(Note: Sections of this book were in different parts of the volume -right-side up, upside-down, back to front and vice versa.)

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Methodist Episcopal Church of Corning records, #6079. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Painted Post (N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs.

controlaccess.subject: Temperance and religion -- New York (State) -- Corning.
Church discipline.
Antislavery movements.
Methodist Episcopal Church -- New York (State) -- Corning.

did.unittitle: Records of the Painted Post Circuit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Note: See the Collection Description for a breakdown of contents.) Items of note are as follows:

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inside cover contains a steward's record listing the number of adults and children and the budget for 1835. Contains meeting minutes for October 9, 1842.

did.unittitle: Section B: Coming Circuit Leader's Meetings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A committee is appointed to build a parsonage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports of a Sunday School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to form a tract society and to promote the cause of temperance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to form a Benevolent Society to include sabbath schools, tract and missionary work and other purposes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to form a missionary society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolution on supporting temperance societies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolution concerning "Wesley's Rule of Discipline" on the use of ardent spirits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last Meeting

did.unittitle: Section E: Quarterly Conference Minutes for the Corning Circuit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion of recent division of the circuit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Curriculum for prospective elders and deacons includes English grammar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: License denied to a man charged with trifling with the affections of a female.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolution on slavery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collection taken for the Wesleyan Genesee Seminary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Member required to give a statement "in love feast".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The church requests a conference to divide the circuit and to make a station of Corning with Caton as an appointment. Painted Post and Campbell Circuit would have Erwin appointed with it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The first of a series of yearly reports on Sunday schools.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports on a dispute over the pastor which has been disrupting the church.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Last entry.

did.unittitle: Section F: Quarterly Meeting Minutes for the M.E. Church of Corning

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Resolved to join with Lawrenceville to hold a camp meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports of an improvement in spirituality and new converts .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After four pages of printed "General Rules of the United Methodist Societies" are lists of members and their attendance at class meetings (1839-1844). The front and back inside covers contain exhortations.

did.unittitle: Part A: Trustee's Annual Meeting Minutes,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records the annual election of trustees and matters pertaining to the temporal life of the church. Items of note include:

did.unittitle: September-October 1860 : Plans to build a new church.
After June 21, 1864 is inserted the "Berea lesson for 1874"
Part B: Quarterly Conference Meeting Minutes ( 1862-? )

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of baptisms includes date, name, residence, officiating minister and witnesses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of marriages includes date, names of parties and their place of residence, officiating minister and witnesses. [note: after section II there is a history of the construction of the church with statistics and a list of "Receipts for the church," September 1862.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record for probationers (1856-1862) includes date, name, condition, residence, church relationship and remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Membership lists (alphabetical) includes name, residence, time and mode of joining, class leader and remarks. Continued after section V.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of the classes lists class members and remarks under the leader's name.
